name: Make-Ahead Cheese and Chile Souffle
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons bottled hot chile salsa, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly butter an 8-cup soufflé dish; set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, melt butter. Whisk in flour until mixture is smooth. Whisk in milk.
3. Whisk sauce over medium-high heat until it boils and thickens. Remove pan from heat.
4. Add cheese, salsa, and salt; stir until cheese melts. Beat in egg yolks.
5. In a large bowl of electric mixer, beat egg whites until they hold soft, moist peaks. Stir one-fourth of the whites into the cheese mixture to lighten it; fold in the remaining whites.
6. Pour mixture into prepared soufflé dish and bake in preheated oven for 30 to 40 minutes.
Timesaver Tip: To make ahead:
- Prepare recipe through step 4. Cover the surface of the mixture with wax paper and refrigerate up to 24 hours. Put egg whites in a bowl, cover, and refrigerate.
- To complete the dish, bring cheese mixture and egg whites to room temperature (this can be done quickly in a microwave oven at a low setting) and continue with steps 5 and 6. Refrigeration is not necessary if the recipe is prepared only a few hours in advance.
To make ahead and freeze:
- Prepare recipe through step 5. Wrap the freezer-to-oven soufflé dish in aluminum foil. Label, date, and freeze up to 2 months. To serve, preheat oven to 375°F. Remove foil and put the soufflé dish directly into the oven from the freezer; do not thaw. Bake for 50 to 55 minutes. Souffle will be puffy and the top well browned. Serve immediately.
